entities.name: BIOLOGICAL NITROGEN FIXATION (BNF)
entities.description: The biological process by which atmospheric nitrogen is converted into ammonia by legumes.

entities.name: LEGUMES
entities.description: Plants of the family Fabaceae capable of fixing nitrogen.

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: A chemical element essential for plant growth and recycled in cropping systems.

entities.name: CROPPING SYSTEMS
entities.description: The agricultural arrangement of crops grown on a piece of land.

entities.name: ISOTOPIC DILUTION (ID) METHOD
entities.description: A highly accurate but expensive and time-consuming technique for measuring biological nitrogen fixation.

entities.name: TOTAL NITROGEN DIFFERENCE (TND) METHOD
entities.description: A simple but less accurate technique for measuring biological nitrogen fixation.

entities.name: COWPEA
entities.description: Vigna unguiculata, the specific legume studied in the research.

entities.name: BURKINA FASO
entities.description: The country in West Africa where the study was conducted.

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: The geographical region where Burkina Faso is located.

entities.name: TOTAL NITROGEN ACCUMULATED
entities.description: The total amount of nitrogen accumulated by plant genotypes.

entities.name: NITROGEN DERIVED FROM THE ATMOSPHERE (NDFA)
entities.description: The proportion of total nitrogen in the plant that originates from the atmosphere.

entities.name: POLYNOMIAL REGRESSION
entities.description: A statistical method used to describe the relationship between N-accumulated and Ndfa-ID.

relationships.description: models the relationship with total N accumulated
